source.context: Aura profile feature was implemented and tested live. Old SavedVariables written by a partial/broken previous implementation caused cascading nil errors at OnLoad time: RuntimeConfiguration.Auras, .Enabled, and .Debug were all nil after LoadCharacterSettings returned, and AuraShares crashed iterating data.Auras. Adding nil guards at the load boundary and at every pairs/ipairs call site resolved all errors.

confidence.rationale: All errors were reproduced at runtime by the user and resolved immediately by the nil guards described. Root cause (SavedVariables written by broken code persisting partial keys) is a well-known WAR addon pattern.

claims.statement: WAR's SavedVariables engine persists partial or malformed config tables across sessions. If a previous version of an addon wrote an incomplete table (missing expected sub-keys), the next version receives the partial table from LoadCharacterSettings and all missing keys are nil — even though the top-level character entry exists. Nil checks on the entry itself are insufficient.
claims.guidance: After calling the character-profile load function (e.g. AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings), validate every expected sub-key and replace nil values with their defaults before using them. Guard at least: RuntimeAuras (init to {}), RuntimeSettings (copy DefaultSettings), Enabled (copy DefaultConfiguration.Enabled), Debug (copy DefaultConfiguration.Debug). This is the correct boundary — not inside the library.

claims.statement: Any Lua function that iterates a WAR addon config table with pairs() or ipairs() must guard the table for nil before the call, even if the table was initialized earlier. SavedVariables corruption or partial-load scenarios mean the table can be nil at any call site that runs before a full validation pass.
claims.guidance: Add an explicit `if table == nil then return end` (or equivalent early-return) before every pairs()/ipairs() call site that touches config data loaded from SavedVariables. Affected patterns in Aura: PrepareSettingsForRuntime, CleanInternalMembers (ipairs over RuntimeAuras), CreateListDisplayData (pairs over RuntimeAuras).

claims.statement: AuraShares iterates AuraAddon.Configuration for all characters and accesses data.Auras. Not all character entries are guaranteed to have an Auras sub-table — entries written by a broken or partial implementation will have a nil Auras key. The guard must be: `if( data ~= nil and data.Auras ~= nil )` — not just `if( data ~= nil )`.
claims.guidance: In any AuraShares loop over the per-character config table, check both the entry and the Auras sub-table before iterating. Failure to guard data.Auras produces a `attempt to call a nil value (pairs)` crash at runtime.

evidence.summary: At game load, AuraSettings.lua:114 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because RuntimeAuras was nil. Root cause: AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings returned a config entry missing the Auras key (written by old broken code). Fix: after OnLoad assignment, guard `if AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras == nil then AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras = {} end`.

ButtonSetPressedFlag was called with nil as second argument because RuntimeConfiguration.Enabled and .Debug were nil. Same root cause (partial SavedVariables). Fix: guard both fields falling back to DefaultConfiguration values immediately after OnLoad assignment.

AuraAddon.lua:200 crashed with 'ipairs(nil)' in PrepareSettingsForRuntime because RuntimeAuras was nil at that call site. Fix: early-return guard at the top of PrepareSettingsForRuntime and CleanInternalMembers.
AuraSettings.lua CreateListDisplayData crashed with 'pairs(nil)'. Same pattern as PrepareSettingsForRuntime. All config-iterating functions need nil guards on RuntimeAuras.

AuraShares.lua:135 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because data.Auras was nil for a stale character entry. The existing check `if( data ~= nil )` was insufficient. Fix: added `and data.Auras ~= nil` to the condition.

promotion.notes: Promote as Lua conventions guidance. The SavedVariables partial-key pitfall affects any WAR addon that evolves its data schema across versions. This is a broadly applicable defensive coding pattern.
criteria: The SavedVariables-partial-key pattern should be promoted as a general Lua caveat.
The AuraShares-specific claim can be scoped to Aura only.
